Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dcaegen2-collectors-ves-master-verify-java/367

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Before disabling configurationPoller, need to move the logic around collector.keystore.alias value resolution into VES application. Currently CBS fetches all config and overwrite collector.properties and configpoller was getting triggered (which inturn called appController.sh restart to set collector.keystore.alias).

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

The updateKeystore() under bin/appController.sh logic should be ported into application for completely disabling the configurationPoller.sh. The collector.keystore.alias should be set (along with other secure property flag) when header.authflag is enabled.

Patch Set 15: Code-Review-1

Noticed some of the original issues still unresolved. 

When collector.properties has invalid collector.keystore.alias, the application errors (Caused by: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Alias name [invalid] does not identify a key entry). 
The expectation is the application should ignore this provided value in the property file and compute it based on collector.keystore.file.location and collector.keystore.passwordfile (this was earlier handled part of the script – appController.sh – updateKeystore method).

As suggested in email, we could remove the collector.keystore.alias also from collector.properties (in addition to consul k-v) and treat it as internal application variable (and log it each time application settings are modified).

To remove collector.keystore.alias from Consul kv - we need to modify the corresponding blueprint (under blueprint repo) and component spec file (under ves repo)
